mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in real-time retail sales indicates improving household demand and near-term revenue growth for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value for 2010-11-01 was 378706.0 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if months with positive 3-month RSAFS momentum do not deliver higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than months with negative momentum, or if the walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor income and weaker consumption expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over the latest 3 monthly observations
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage value for 2010-11-01 was 9.8 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if triggered returns are not lower than non-triggered returns at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.5% and consumer inflation is not accelerating, the low discount-rate channel dominates, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth is below 3%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value for 2010-11-01 was 0.19 as of the decision date
fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value for 2010-11-01 was 219.146 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate and low-inflation state has an average 60-day S&P 500 return that is not positive, or if its hit rate is not above the unconditional positive-return frequency.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production signals weaker operating leverage and earnings revisions for cyclical firms, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls for 2 consecutive monthly observations or declines by more than 0.5% over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value for 2010-11-01 was 93.8961 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO contractions are followed by non-negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts from a depressed level signals improving credit transmission, construction demand, and household balance-sheet confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is below 700 and rises by more than 5% over the latest 3 monthly observations
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value for 2010-10-01 was 519.0 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if depressed-level HOUST rebounds do not produce higher average 60-day S&P 500 returns than depressed-level non-rebounds, or if the return spread is negative over two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth accompanied by a falling unemployment rate confirms broad labor-market repair, raising expected consumption and corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months and UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 percentage points over the same 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value for 2010-11-01 was 130539.0 as of the decision date
fact:UNRATE vintage value for 2010-11-01 was 9.8 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down condition has a lower 20-day average S&P 500 return than PAYEMS-up alone, or if its conditional hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices combined with a rising federal funds rate signals a policy-tightening shock to valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS rises over the latest 3 monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-tightening observations have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A one-month acceleration in industrial production after a weak prior quarter signals an upside growth surprise before quarterly GDP data fully confirm it, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by more than 0.4% month over month while the latest available GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is positive
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage value for 2010-07-01 was 13277.4 as of the decision date
fact:INDPRO vintage value for 2010-11-01 was 93.8961 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the INDPRO acceleration with positive GDPC1 condition fails to produce a positive mean 5-day S&P 500 return, or if its hit rate is not above the unconditional 5-day hit rate.
